caveat: The guarantee is IN EXPECTATION and marginal, under exchangeability and a loss that is non-increasing in lambda — the module verifies monotonicity on the supplied grid and fails closed, but cannot check it off-grid. Where the paper sets lambdahat = lambda_max when no lambda qualifies (assuming L(lambda_max) <= alpha), this module returns None — an honest refusal instead of an unverifiable assumption. Enumerated risk verifies the mechanism on fixed pools, not any deployed model's losses.
statement: The vendored conformal-risk-control library passes all 21 checks with 0 mismatches: the published selection rule lambdahat = inf{lambda : (n/(n+1)) Rhat_n(lambda) + B/(n+1) <= alpha} reproduces 5 hand-computed selections and algebraic bounds, THE RISK THEOREM IS ENUMERATED over the 10 pool-by-alpha combinations satisfying its preconditions (exhaustive leave-one-out risk <= alpha as exact Fractions, for binary and fractional bounded losses), 2 out-of-precondition cases refuse honestly, and with the miscoverage-indicator loss the selection reduces EXACTLY to the split-conformal quantile rule on all 4 alphas.
